metadata.description: Factory-grade technical data sheets for 300 textile weave designs including interlacement matrices, loom specifications, material properties, production parameters, and AI/software encoding

core_structure.interlacement_description: Alternate warp and weft intersections. Binary 1 = warp on top, 0 = weft on top

loom_specifications.shaft_threading_logic: Sequential straight threading through all shafts

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Simple interlacement requires minimal loom coordination

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: Regular, continuous running. ચાલ = uniform speed, no complex patterns
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Single warp beam, standard arrangement. આંટો = straight beam loading

pattern_encoding.graph_representation: Bipartite graph: warp nodes ↔ weft nodes with 1:1 edges

optimization_opportunities.maintain_design: Any modification breaks plain weave definition

1_plain_weave.notes: Plain weave is the foundation of all textile structures. Used globally for 40-50% of all woven fabrics. Simple but versatile.

core_structure.interlacement_description: Paired warp and weft yarns create basket-like appearance

loom_specifications.shaft_threading_logic: Two ends through each shaft in sequence

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Slightly more complex than plain due to paired threading
common_defects_risks: Threading errors if pairs not maintained

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: ચાલ = 4-pick repeat cycle. Smooth, paired progression
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Single warp beam with paired end arrangement

pattern_encoding.matrix_form: 4×4 binary matrix (2×2 repeat base with pairing)
pattern_encoding.graph_representation: Paired bipartite graph with 2:2 edge groups

2_basket_weave.notes: Variant of plain weave with grouped yarns. Creates texture while maintaining simplicity. Popular in casual apparel.

core_structure.interlacement_description: Warp floats dominate (2 picks). Creates vertical ridge effect

loom_specifications.drafting_style: Grouped Draft (paired ends through each shaft)
loom_specifications.shaft_threading_logic: Two ends through shaft 1, two through shaft 2, repeat

reed_and_density.density_note: High EPI (for vertical ribs), lower PPI

cramming_info.reason: Rib effect comes from yarn density, not cramming

visual_characteristics.appearance: Pronounced linear pattern from warp dominance

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Simple interlacement, but higher EPI requires careful tension
common_defects_risks: Rib distortion if EPI/PPI ratio off

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: ચાલ = 2-pick cycle. Vertical progression creates rib effect
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Standard warp beam with paired end density

optimization_opportunities.reduce_yarn_usage: Increase PPI (tighter construction, less warp needed)
optimization_opportunities.improve_speed: Already simple; can't optimize much further
optimization_opportunities.maintain_design: Keep warp-to-weft float ratio for rib definition

3_rib_weave_warp_rib.notes: Warp-faced rib creates vertical texture. Base for many technical and apparel fabrics. Popular in India for formal suiting.

core_structure.interlacement_description: Diagonal progression +1/-1 step. Creates 45° twill line

lifting_sequence.description: Shaft 3 up → warp 3 shows (shifted right again)

material_aesthetic.texture_feel: Smooth, slightly dense, diagonal hand feel

float_analysis.float_pattern: 2 warp over 2 weft in diagonal progression

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Straightforward diagonal progression. No complex floating.

cost_factors.yarn_consumption: ~5-10% less than plain weave (due to float efficiency)

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: ચાલ = 4-pick diagonal cycle. Continuous progression for twill
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Standard warp beam with even tension across 3 shafts

pattern_encoding.graph_representation: Directed acyclic graph showing diagonal progression

variation_logic.shift_pattern: Reverse direction (left-hand twill instead of right-hand)

optimization_opportunities.reduce_yarn_usage: Increase PPI (tighter picks, less warp floats)
optimization_opportunities.improve_speed: Optimize shaft cycling for loom speed
optimization_opportunities.maintain_design: Keep diagonal step consistent for clarity

4_twill_weave_regular.notes: 2/2 Twill is the most common twill structure. Base for denim, chinos, and many apparel fabrics. Right-hand (RH) or left-hand (LH) variations common.

core_structure.interlacement_description: Single intersection per repeat. Long warp floats (4) create lustrous face

loom_specifications.drafting_style: Straight Draft (or Point Draft for symmetry)

cramming_info.reason: Satin structure already creates smooth surface with floats

end_use_applications: Apparel (sarees, bridal wear, evening gowns, blouses)

visual_characteristics.appearance: Smooth, reflective surface due to long warp floats

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: 5-shaft coordination required; long floats need careful handling

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: ચાલ = 5-pick satin cycle. Special attention needed for float management
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: 5-shaft warp beam arrangement with careful tension distribution

pattern_encoding.graph_representation: Cyclic directed graph with single intersections

variation_logic.modify_float: Adjust step parameter (3-end = shorter floats, 4-end = longer)
variation_logic.shift_pattern: Mirror satin (weft-faced sateen instead of warp-faced)

optimization_opportunities.maintain_design: Keep step=4 for pure satin effect

5_satin_weave_5_end.notes: 5-end satin is industry standard for lustrous, smooth fabrics. Critical for sarees, bridal fabrics, and luxury goods. Requires careful production control.

core_structure.interlacement_description: Two parallel twill lines. Thicker floats create bold diagonal

cramming_info.reason: Double twill creates sufficient texture without cramming

material_aesthetic.texture_feel: Bold texture, slightly stiff, pronounced diagonal

visual_characteristics.appearance: Strong diagonal with increased depth perception

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: 4-shaft coordination straightforward; similar to regular 2/2 twill

pattern_encoding.matrix_form: 4×8 binary matrix with 2/2 twill base doubled

variation_logic.shift_pattern: Create zigzag effect with alternating direction

101_double_twill.notes: Popular for formal suiting and heavy apparel. Double twill creates premium appearance with good durability.

core_structure.interlacement_matrix: [[Complex 32×32 binary matrix with floral pattern]] - See detailed pattern encoding below
core_structure.interlacement_description: Complex patterned weave with floral motifs. Selective shaft lifting creates image

wif_structure.notes: Requires jacquard loom or electronic dobby

loom_specifications.shaft_threading_logic: Uniform threading across all 16 shafts for pattern flexibility

peg_plan.lifting_sequence: Programmed based on pattern image. Each pick lifts specific shaft combination

warp.fiber_type: Silk, Cotton, Polyester (often dyed for color)

cramming_info.reason: Jacquard patterns benefit from tight construction for image clarity

material_aesthetic.texture_feel: Smooth to textured depending on pattern, luxurious

visual_characteristics.appearance: Complex multi-colored or tonal floral design

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Complex pattern coordination limits speed compared to simple weaves

cost_factors.estimated_cost: +50-150% (vs. plain weave, depending on pattern complexity)

surat_factory_terminology.chaal: ચાલ = Pattern-programmed running. Traditional hand jacquard needs 'chaal' operator for lever pulling
surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Multiple color warp beams if multi-color design. Careful beam tension balancing

pattern_encoding.matrix_form: 32×32 binary matrix (or higher for fine details)
pattern_encoding.graph_representation: Pattern graph with motif nodes and interlacement edges

optimization_opportunities.maintain_design: Preserve pattern fidelity at different resolutions

201_floral_jacquard.notes: Floral jacquard is signature fabric in Indian textiles, especially Banarasi sarees. Requires skilled loom operators and careful quality control. High value-add product.

core_structure.interlacement_matrix: Complex 40×40 binary matrix with reversible pattern
core_structure.interlacement_description: Reversible damask. Same pattern appears on both sides (opposed floats)

wif_structure.colorbook: Single color (contrast from weave structure alone)
wif_structure.notes: Reversible pattern = same image both sides

peg_plan.lifting_sequence: Symmetrical: first 20 picks mirror the last 20 picks

cramming_info.reason: Tight cramming ensures pattern clarity on both sides

visual_characteristics.surface_effect: Pattern visible from both sides due to opposed floats

industrial_production.speed_reasoning: Complex reversible pattern requires careful shaft coordination
common_defects_risks: Reversibility failure (pattern not matching both sides)

surat_factory_terminology.anto_beam_setup: Fine warp beam with extremely even tension

202_damask.notes: Damask is premium formal fabric. Reversibility requires expert engineering. Traditional damask = UNESCO cultural heritage (Damascus region origin). High-end market product.
